payload.summary: Planned alignment of repo vocabulary, validator surfaces, vendor-diversity policy, and frontend-validation coverage with the Factory Missions architecture. Decomposed into 7 scope-isolated work packages plus wp-contracts and wp-integration. WP4 reduced from 'add gen-eval phase' to 'extend existing template-only handler' after discovering the phase already exists at validate-feature/SKILL.md:30,260-307. Coordinated tier: WP1, WP2, WP6 parallel-safe; WP3→WP4→WP5→WP7 form a serial chain on the gen-eval extension path.

decisions.title: Single umbrella change vs roadmap of 4 small changes
decisions.rationale: One plan-approval gate + one PR keeps human-attention cost bounded; cross-WP dependencies become intra-change contracts in contracts/

decisions.title: Playwright validator packaged as a peer skill, not a new gen-eval mode
decisions.rationale: Different system deps (npx, browsers); peer-skill packaging matches repo convention and keeps gen-eval Python-only

decisions.title: behavioral_failure as a peer enum value, not a metadata discriminator on correctness
decisions.rationale: JSON Schema validation can route on type-enum values; metadata discriminators are harder to filter on

decisions.title: Vendor-diversity policy is per-change, not per-package
decisions.rationale: Implementable from existing change-id session state; per-package would force vendor-pool exhaustion on changes with many packages

decisions.title: Existing template-only gen-eval phase preserved as fallback path
decisions.rationale: Backward compat; reduces conflict surface with harness-engineering-features which also touches validate-feature/SKILL.md

decisions.title: Sample frontend uses static HTML, not a JS framework
decisions.rationale: Smallest possible surface; proves validator-agnosticism; no app-side npm deps in CI

decisions.title: This proposal merges before harness-engineering-features
decisions.rationale: Operator-selected; harness-engineering-features rebases onto our additive sections (no edits to existing content reduces conflict risk)

next_steps: Land wp-contracts first (smallest, gates everything else)
Run WP1, WP2, WP6 in parallel after wp-contracts (independent write scopes)
Run WP3→WP4→WP5→WP7 serially (each depends on prior schema/output)
Run wp-integration last; verify harness-engineering-features rebases cleanly

relevant_files.description: TDD-ordered task list with spec/contract/decision references per task
9 packages (incl. wp-contracts, wp-integration); all validations green
